subject: Our bare repos need to have casablanca branch
commit_message:
Our bare repos need to have casablanca branch

This is required to align with OOM charts, otherwise
some components like DMAAP might be not initialized
properly. Furthmore removing duplicated files, most
of our data_lists are same for Casablanca MR and
Casablanca base releases.

Change-Id: I376410c7915d8f2682287c31392e2f3796adc990
Issue-ID: OOM-1771
Signed-off-by:  Michal Ptacek <m.ptacek@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-yaml-lint/111/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-yaml-lint/111/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-yaml-lint/111

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

What is the logic? As tags are more stable (do not change) than branches.

Patch Set 1:

> What is the logic? As tags are more stable (do not change) than
 > branches.
primarily we should be aligned with OOM charts. Furthermore in messageservice case 3.0.1 tag looks like something not really related to casablanca branch. In Dublin it sounds to me that it's better to have latest commit from stable branch than rely on tags. Tags handling does not work on this project. Some repos even don't have them, some have them on wrong commit  ... so using latest commits from stable branch sounds like way to go.

Patch Set 1:

> > What is the logic? As tags are more stable (do not change) than
 > > branches.
 > primarily we should be aligned with OOM charts. Furthermore in
 > messageservice case 3.0.1 tag looks like something not really
 > related to casablanca branch. In Dublin it sounds to me that it's
 > better to have latest commit from stable branch than rely on tags.
 > Tags handling does not work on this project. Some repos even don't
 > have them, some have them on wrong commit  ... so using latest
 > commits from stable branch sounds like way to go.

that's ok, but as mentioned tags are more stable. Branch is never stable.

Patch Set 1:

name of the file is the tag name...

Patch Set 1:

> name of the file is the tag name...
yes it is. I don't think we need to change it on that level. The original idea was to offer 3.0.0 version of original casablanca and 3.0.1 version of casablanca with service pack ... due to wrong handling of those tags we better have there latest casablanca code

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

files with 3.0.x does not mean anything anymore as only one version for those in git branch. Git branch is defining the release.

Patch Set 3:

> files with 3.0.x does not mean anything anymore as only one version
 > for those in git branch. Git branch is defining the release.

all datalists are casablanca, we have 3.0.0 for base casab., 3.0.1 for casab MR ...  to me i will looks quite messy if we keep branch name for some data lists and tags for another data lists ... I like 3.0.x more :-) I dont think people will get confused by this

Patch Set 3:

> > files with 3.0.x does not mean anything anymore as only one
 > version
 > > for those in git branch. Git branch is defining the release.
 > 
 > all datalists are casablanca, we have 3.0.0 for base casab., 3.0.1
 > for casab MR ...  to me i will looks quite messy if we keep branch
 > name for some data lists and tags for another data lists ... I like
 > 3.0.x more :-) I dont think people will get confused by this

no branch name anywhere if it's just one version in release. Like is't now for those git files.
Just onap_git_repos.list is the filename because git branch itself defines the version. 
For docker images we have 2 versions within same git branch.
